mnemonics: POWER (Performance, Optimization, Workload, Efficiency, Resources) - A framework for remembering the key levers to reduce runtime energy consumption.
keyPoints: Runtime energy is primarily consumed by the CPU, memory, and disk I/O operations.
Optimizing algorithms and code efficiency directly reduces the energy required for computation.
Dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) is a key technique for saving energy by adjusting processor power based on workload.
commonMistakes: Assuming that faster code always equals more energy-efficient code; sometimes running slower at a lower power state is more efficient.
Focusing only on hardware efficiency while ignoring the massive energy savings available through software and algorithm optimization.
